content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of size NNN. He can perform the following operation on AAA:


Select an iii (1‚â§i‚â§N)(1 \le i \le N)(1‚â§i‚â§N) and for all 1‚â§j‚â§i1 \le j \le i1‚â§j‚â§i, set Aj:=Aj+1A_j := A_j + 1Aj‚Äã:=Aj‚Äã+1 (i.e. add 111 to every element in the prefix of length iii).
Chef wants to convert AAA to a palindrome by using the above operation **minimum** number of times. Can you help Chef?

If it is not possible to convert AAA to a palindrome, output ‚àí1-1‚àí1.

Note: An array is called palindrome if it reads the same backwards and forwards, for e.g. [1,4,1][1, 4, 1][1,4,1] and [7,3,3,7][7, 3, 3, 7][7,3,3,7] are palindromic arrays.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚Äî the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚Äî the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **minimum** number of operations required to convert AAA to a palindromic array.


If it is not possible to do so, output ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The given array is already a palindrome.
Test case 222: It can be proven that it is not possible to convert AAA to a palindromic array using the given operation.
Test case 333: We can perform the following operations:

Select i=1i = 1i=1, [1,2,3,4]‚Üí[2,2,3,4][1, 2, 3, 4] \rightarrow [2, 2, 3, 4][1,2,3,4]‚Üí[2,2,3,4]
Select i=2i = 2i=2, [2,2,3,4]‚Üí[3,3,3,4][2, 2, 3, 4] \rightarrow [3, 3, 3, 4][2,2,3,4]‚Üí[3,3,3,4]
Select i=1i = 1i=1, [3,3,3,4]‚Üí[4,3,3,4][3, 3, 3, 4] \rightarrow [4, 3, 3, 4][3,3,3,4]‚Üí[4,3,3,4]
